title: Key Jan. 6 witness says lawyer sought to influence testimony
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former White House aide Cassidy Hutchinson told the House Jan. 6 committee that her first lawyer advised her against being fully forthcoming with the panel, telling her it was acceptable to testify that she did not recall certain events when she actually did and that “the less you remember, the better,” according to a transcript of one of her interviews released Thursday.

title: Trump’s taxes: House OKs bill on IRS audits of presidents
shortDescription: WASHINGON (AP) — The House took action Thursday after disclosures that the IRS never full reviewed Donald Trump’s tax returns during his presidency, passing a bill that would require audits of any president’s income tax filings.

title: Senate reaches deal on $1.7T package, pushing toward passage
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate appeared back on track Thursday to pass a $1.7 trillion bill to finance federal agencies through September and provide roughly $45 billion in military and economic assistance to Ukraine after lawmakers reached agreement on a final series of votes.

title: White House: Russia’s Wagner received arms from North Korea
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House said Thursday that the Wagner Group, a private Russian military company, has taken delivery of an arms shipment from North Korea to help bolster its forces as it fights side-by-side with Russian troops in Ukraine.

title: Social secretary helps ‘people’s house’ welcome the people
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Cookies forgotten in purses until they crumble. Complaints about not being invited. Boycotts over political disagreements with President Joe Biden.

title: IRS mandatory presidential audit policy goes under spotlight
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — An IRS policy governing the audits of tax returns filed by U.S. presidents is under new scrutiny after a report published by a congressional panel found the agency failed to perform the mandatory inspection of Donald Trump's returns until Congress pressed for information about the process.

title: Spanish govt reforms sedition law in nod to Catalan allies
shortDescription: MADRID (AP) — Spain on Thursday sealed final approval for controversial reforms to the crimes of sedition and embezzlement, a move by the ruling center-left coalition to retain the support of a pro-independence Catalan party and ease tensions in the northeastern region.

title: Germany nationalizes gas supplier Uniper after EU approval
shortDescription: BERLIN (AP) — The German government said Thursday that it has nationalized energy company Uniper after the European Union gave its blessing for it to rescue the gas supplier.The government announced its plan to nationalize Uniper in September, expanding state intervention in the energy sector to prevent a shortage resulting from Russia’s war in Ukraine.

title: Fiji calls in military after close election is disputed
shortDescription: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Fijian police on Thursday said they were calling in the military to help maintain security following a close election last week that is now being disputed.It was an alarming development in a Pacific nation where democracy remains fragile and there have been four military coups in the past 35 years.

title: Kari Lake trial to be Arizona governor lacks day 1 shockers
shortDescription: PHOENIX (AP) — Since her loss in the Arizona governor’s race, Republican Kari Lake has claimed the election was stolen and promised bombshells showing she was the rightful winner. But on the first day of trial in her election challenge Wednesday, her lawyers alleged small-bore problems that didn't appear to show the widespread, intentional misconduct she would need to prove.

title: Senate hits snag in bid to pass $1.7 trillion spending bill
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Momentum toward passing a $1.7 trillion government funding bill with more aid for Ukraine slowed considerably Wednesday as lawmakers struggled to reach an agreement on amendments needed to get to a final vote and avoid a partial government shutdown at midnight Friday.

title: Ardern, rival turn her hot-mic vulgarity into charity’s win
shortDescription: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — When New Zealand Prime Minister Jacinda Ardern was caught on a hot mic using a vulgarity against a rival politician last week, it seemed the nation's political discourse could be taking an ugly turn heading into an election year.

title: Panel recommends $474M heating aid package after hearing
shortDescription: AUGUSTA, Maine (AP) — A hastily assembled legislative panel voted unanimously Wednesday to approve a proposed $474 million heating assistance package following a public hearing that was demanded by Senate Republicans.

title: Analysis: Biden, Zelenskyy try to keep Congress from balking
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Volodymyr Zelenskyy's dramatic visit to Washington was a moment for the White House to demonstrate to Russia's Vladimir Putin that the United States would sustain its commitment to the war for, as President Joe Biden put it, “as long as it takes.”

title: Pageantry evoking Churchill greets Zelenskyy in Washington
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — At a time of grave consequence, a wartime leader crossed the Atlantic to arrive at a White House decked in holiday decor to consult with the American president about a war in Europe.

title: As flu rages, US releases medicine from national stockpile
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Wednesday it will release doses of prescription flu medicine from the Strategic National Stockpile to states as flu-sickened patients continue to flock to hospitals and doctors' offices around the country.

title: Jan. 6 panel prepares to unveil final report on insurrection
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — An 800-page report set to be released Thursday by House investigators will conclude that then-President Donald Trump criminally plotted to overturn his 2020 election defeat and “provoked his supporters to violence” at the Capitol with false claims of widespread voter fraud.

title: Newspaper: Georgia congressman may have illegally voted
shortDescription: ATLANTA (AP) — A Georgia congressman voted three times this year in a county where he no longer lives, possibly violating state law, state records show.Republican U.S. Rep. Drew Ferguson's congressional website shows he lives with his wife in The Rock, 63 miles away from his former home in West Point.

title: EXPLAINER: How the latest US aid makes Ukraine stronger
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The presence of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy in Washington on Wednesday puts a dramatic exclamation point on the Biden administration's latest announcement of military aid to the war-torn nation.

title: Emmett Till and his mother honored with congressional medal
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House unanimously passed a bill Wednesday to posthumously award the Congressional Gold Medal to Emmett Till, the Chicago teenager murdered by white supremacists in the 1950s, and his mother, Mamie Till-Mobley.

title: Senate confirms new U.S. ambassador to Russia
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate voted overwhelmingly Wednesday to confirm the new U.S. ambassador to Russia.Hours before Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy was expected to arrive in Washington for a historic visit, senators voted to 93-2 to confirm veteran diplomat Lynne M.

title: US sending Patriots to Ukraine under $1.85B aid package
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration announced Wednesday that it will provide $1.85 billion in military aid to Ukraine, rolling out funding for a Patriot missile battery as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy arrived in Washington for his first known trip out of his country since Russia invaded in February.

title: US hits more Iranian officials with human rights sanctions
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration on Wednesday slapped sanctions on Iran’s chief prosecutor, four other Iranian officials and a company that supports the country’s security forces for their roles in an ongoing violent crackdown on antigovernment protests.

title: States contend with short timeline to correct broadband map
shortDescription: LOS ANGELES (AP) — States are racing against a deadline to challenge the map federal officials will use to divvy up the nation's largest-ever investment in high-speed internet.At stake is a share of the $42.5 billion Broadband Equity, Access and Deployment program, part of the infrastructure measure President Joe Biden signed into law last year.

title: Chief justice in Ohio map flap: Court attacks harm democracy
shortDescription: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Ohio Chief Justice Maureen O'Connor isn't rattled on a personal level by the political attacks she endured from fellow Republicans during Ohio's protracted redistricting fight.

title: EXPLAINER: How Trump ignored advisers, spread election lies
shortDescription: The executive summary of the House Jan.

title: The AP Interview: Vermont Sen. Leahy ponders his legacy
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Sen. Patrick Leahy lingered on a narrow balcony on the west side of the U.S. Capitol, soaking in a panoramic view of the National Mall, the Washington Monument and, beyond, the Lincoln Memorial.

title: Rule delay makes big EV tax credit possible early next year
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — People who want to buy an electric vehicle could get a bigger-than-expected tax credit come Jan. 1 because of a delay by the Treasury Department in drawing up rules for the tax breaks.

title: 'Alive and kicking': Ukraine president Zelenskyy presses Congress for more aid
shortDescription: Biden promised staunch support for Ukraine as he welcomed Volodymyr Zelenskyy to the White House during the Ukraine leader's whirlwind trip to the US.

title: Zelenskyy and Biden meet at White House
shortDescription: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy visited the U.S. in his first foreign trip since war began in his country.

title: Joe Biden welcomes Zelenskyy at White House
shortDescription: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's visit to the U.S. comes as Joe Biden and Congress reaffirm their commitment to Ukraine.

title: Notable dignitaries who have addressed Congress
shortDescription: Visiting foreign leaders address Joint Meetings of Congress often during state visits to the United States.

title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy to meet with Biden, address Congress
shortDescription: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy will make his first trip to Washington since Russia began its violent invasion of Ukraine in February.

title: Supreme Court denies Trump effort to keep tax returns from House panel
shortDescription: Trump's record at the Supreme Court is not particularly good, even though he nominated three of the court's current associate justices.

title: House panel votes to release Trump tax information
shortDescription: The House Ways and Means Committee got copies of Donald Trump's tax returns from the Treasury Department a few weeks ago after a long legal battle.

title: House panel votes on Trump's tax returns
shortDescription: The Democrat-led House Ways and Means Committee voted to release some of former President Donald Trump's tax returns in the next few days.

title: Will Hope Hicks' complete Jan. 6 testimony be damning for Trump?
shortDescription: The Jan. 6 committee revealed damaging testimony from Trump loyalist Hope Hicks on Monday. With full report and transcripts coming, does it have more?

title: Biden urges Supreme Court to let it end Title 42 migrant program
shortDescription: Biden's response came a day after Chief Justice Roberts temporarily blocked the Biden administration from ending Title 42 migrant expulsions.

title: OnPolitics: Will Donald Trump's taxes become public?
shortDescription: After years of speculation and legal action, the public may soon get a look at some long-sought information: Donald Trump's tax records.

title: Lawmakers unveil bill to avoid government shutdown, boost Ukraine
shortDescription: Congressional leaders unveiled a $1.7 trillion spending package early Tuesday that includes another large round of aid to Ukraine, a nearly 10% boost in defense spending.

title: Biden to travel to Mexico for meeting with AMLO amid migrant crisis
shortDescription: President Joe Biden's announced visit comes amid a surge in migrants that has taxed border communities and as the U.S. weighs lifting Title 42.

title: Pence says he doesn't think Trump should face criminal charges over Jan. 6
shortDescription: Former Vice President Mike Pence said Donald Trump acted recklessly, but not criminally, on Jan. 6 so should not be charged by the Justice Department.

title: Election law overhaul part of spending bill before Congress
shortDescription: An overhaul of the 1887 Electoral Count Act was included in the spending bill Congress will debate this week.

title: Will Trump's taxes become public? What to expect if House panel votes for release.
shortDescription: The House Committee on Ways and Means meets Tuesday to decide what to do with Trump tax records it obtained after years of legal action.

title: The Jan. 6 report: What remains unknown in the Capitol attack?
shortDescription: The Jan. 6 committee's report Monday left out details on potential witness tampering and a reported clash between Donald Trump and the Secret Service.

title: Unprecedented: Jan. 6 panel votes criminal referral for Trump
shortDescription: After a million documents and a thousand interviews, the committee concludes Trump bears responsibility for the mob and their assault.

title: Supreme Court pauses Biden effort to end Title 42 immigration policy
shortDescription: Title 42 has been used to expel migrants more than 2.4 million times since 2020. GOP-led states want to require the Biden administration to continue.

title: Trump criminal referrals: Inciting insurrection could be hardest to prove
shortDescription: Jan. 6 committee members pointed to Trump's provocative address on the Ellipse when he took no action during the next three hours to stop the assault.

title: Jan. 6 panel urges DOJ charge Trump over efforts to undo 2020 election: recap
shortDescription: Congress' 18-month investigation into former President Donald Trump's role in the Jan. 6, 2021 U.S. Capitol attack will come to a close this week.

title: OnPolitics: Jan. 6 committee urges DOJ charge Trump in Capitol attack
shortDescription: The House panel on Jan. 6 recommends the Justice Department prosecute Donald Trump for his efforts to overturn the 2020 election in its final report.

title: It Took Just Five Months to Lose Her Rights to Her Kids Forever
shortDescription: Twenty-five years ago, Congress passed a law aimed at speeding up adoptions of children languishing in foster care. In the process, it destroyed hundreds of thousands of families through the termination of parental rights.

title: Congress and Industry Leaders Call for Crackdown on Hospice Fraud
shortDescription: Following a ProPublica-New Yorker investigation into the hospice industry, members of the Comprehensive Care Caucus and national trade groups are demanding reform.

title: Congress Passes Bill to Rein in Conflicts of Interest for Consultants Such as McKinsey
shortDescription: President Joe Biden is expected to sign the legislation, whose Senate sponsors cited ProPublica’s reporting on McKinsey’s conflicts in working for both the FDA and opioid makers like Purdue Pharma.

title: Senate Moves to Pass Spending Package After Immigration Dispute Initially Delayed Votes
shortDescription: An effort to force a vote to maintain Title 42, a pandemic-era border policy, had stalled the scramble to pass the spending package before a midnight Friday deadline.By Emily Cochrane

title: Full Transcript of Zelensky’s Speech Before Congress
shortDescription: The Ukrainian president delivered an emotional appeal for further American support, vowing that his country would prevail in its war with Russia.

title: Did George Santos Also Mislead Voters About His Jewish Descent?
shortDescription: Genealogy websites cited by The Forward suggest that Mr. Santos’s grandparents were born in Brazil and were not “Holocaust refugees,” as he has claimed.By Michael Gold and Grace Ashford

title: President Biden Signs Bill Outlawing Private Ownership of Big Cats
shortDescription: The new law also forbids public petting of these animals, as shown in the Netflix series “Tiger King.”By Christine Chung

title: What Really Saved the Democrats This Year?
shortDescription: Progressives and moderates both have a theory of the case. By Thomas B. Edsall

title: Senate kicks government funding drama closer to shutdown deadline
shortDescription: Lawmakers are at a standstill over a proposed GOP amendment tied to a Trump-era border policy, which could force Democrats to take a politically tricky vote.
